---
title: "PhD Graduate Student Experience"
subtitle: "A comprehensive resource hub for navigating graduate school"
description: "Practical guides covering salary, wealth building, family planning, health insurance, and career ROI for PhD students."
page-layout: full
toc: false
---

::: {.hero-banner}
# Welcome

This repository is a **comprehensive toolkit** for PhD students, combining academic-work templates with practical resources to improve every dimension of the graduate student experience — from fair compensation and financial wellness to family planning, healthcare, and career readiness.
:::

---

## Resource Guides

Navigate the guides using the sidebar, or jump directly to a topic below.

::: {.grid .resource-guides-grid}

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6 .resource-guide-group}
### 💰 Compensation & Finances

- **[Salary & Cost of Living](student_experience/salary_and_cost_of_living.qmd)**<br>
  Evaluate your stipend against local cost of living, use city-by-city benchmarks, and learn evidence-based negotiation strategies.
- **[Wealth Building](student_experience/wealth_building.qmd)**<br>
  Monthly budgeting templates, Roth IRA and 403(b) guides, student loan management, and credit-building steps — all calibrated for a grad-school income.
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6 .resource-guide-group}
### 🏥 Life & Wellbeing

- **[Family Planning](student_experience/family_planning.qmd)**<br>
  FMLA and Title IX rights, parental leave policy checklists, childcare cost benchmarks, subsidy programs, and partner career planning.
- **[Health & Health Insurance](student_experience/health_insurance.qmd)**<br>
  Insurance glossary, coverage-gap solutions, mental health resource tiers, crisis hotlines, and advisor-relationship red flags.
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .resource-guide-group}
### 🎓 Career

- **[Career ROI, Contracts & Employment Prospects](student_experience/career_roi.qmd)**<br>
  Opportunity-cost calculator, PhD salary premium by sector, full offer-letter review checklist (compensation, equity, IP assignment, non-compete), negotiation strategy, and postdoc evaluation framework.
:::

:::

---

## How to Use These Guides

Each guide is structured around four components:

| Component | What It Is |
|---|---|
| **Key questions to ask** | What you should know before signing or committing |
| **Reference benchmarks** | Federal minimums, peer comparisons, and industry standards |
| **Action checklists** | Concrete steps you can take today |
| **External links** | Curated tools, calculators, and advocacy organizations |

These documents are living templates — fork the repository, customize them for your institution and situation, and update them as policies change.

---

## Academic Templates

This repository also includes LaTeX templates for academic work. See the [`manuscript/`](https://github.com/mcphersonlab/graduate-school/tree/main/manuscript), [`grants/`](https://github.com/mcphersonlab/graduate-school/tree/main/grants), [`presentations/`](https://github.com/mcphersonlab/graduate-school/tree/main/presentations), and [`protocols/`](https://github.com/mcphersonlab/graduate-school/tree/main/protocols) directories.

This project uses [CRediT (Contributor Roles Taxonomy)](https://credit.niso.org/) to clearly specify the roles and contributions of each individual participant.

---

## Contributing

If you have an update, correction, or additional resource to suggest, please [open an issue](https://github.com/mcphersonlab/graduate-school/issues) or submit a pull request. Improving this repository helps every student who uses it.
